search_summary: Exhaustive search across web sources. Sypik is a Vietnamese brand with limited presence in US/Western paddle databases. The main databases (Matt's Pickleball, JohnKew, Pickleball Effect) are JS-rendered and couldn't be scraped. Most sources only have manufacturer specs (weight, dimensions), not independently tested swingweight/twistweight/power/pop data.

Sypik Avatar Ultimate Pro Tour 16mm.source: balance_point: sportsshop.asia (listed as 9.46 inches = ~240.3mm from butt end). spin_rpm: 1590 RPM from official Sypik packaging and sportsshop.asia listing. Reddit user tested similar Triton Pro 3 at 2300-2400 RPM (suggesting 1590 may understate actual spin). Swingweight described as 'Moderate' by mypickleball.com.au (qualitative only). Reddit comment says Triton Pro 3 tested at 'mid 90th percentiles for power and pop' which suggests Avatar is similar or better.
Sypik Avatar Ultimate Pro Tour 16mm.notes: Gen 4 foam core (SYCORE All-Foam Core). Weight ~7.85-8.13oz (~222-226g). Elongated shape 16.5x7.4in. Handle 5.55in, grip 4.35in circumference.

Sypik Triton Ultimate Pro Tour Gen 3 16m.source: No independently tested stats found. Only manufacturer specs available from retailer sites. Reddit user tested spin at 2300-2400 RPM (this was specifically for this paddle). Same user said it tested at 'mid 90th percentiles for power and pop' on their testing setup.
Sypik Triton Ultimate Pro Tour Gen 3 16m.notes: Honeycomb core. Weight 7.8-8.2oz. T700 Carbon surface. 16.5x7.4in. Handle 5.55in, grip 4.35in.

Sypik Triton 5 Ultimate Pro 16mm.source: No independently tested stats found. Only manufacturer specs from spinwavepickleball.com and sportsdeal.asia.
Sypik Triton 5 Ultimate Pro 16mm.notes: New Honeycomb Core (upgraded from Gen 3). Upgraded Raw Carbon Surface for more spin. Weight 8.0oz. 16.5x7.4in. Handle 5.55in, grip 4.35in. Metallic Gradient Edge.

Sypik Triton Pro Gen 2.source: No independently tested stats found. Only manufacturer specs from pickleplayglobal.com and sypik.com. Reddit user praised it for 'really good swing weight' but no number given.
Sypik Triton Pro Gen 2.notes: Polymer Honeycomb core. T700 Carbon surface. Weight 8.0oz. 16.5x7.4in. Handle 5.55in, grip 4.35in. 16mm thickness.

recommendation: The specific numeric stats (swingweight, twistweight, power mph, pop mph) are likely only available in the Matt's Pickleball and JohnKew databases which are JS-rendered. To get these, someone would need to manually look them up on those sites, or check if these Sypik paddles have even been tested by those labs. Sypik being a Vietnamese brand, they may not yet be in Western paddle databases. The only concrete numbers found were: Avatar balance point (240.3mm/9.46in) and spin RPM (1590 official, ~2300-2400 user-tested for Triton Gen 3).
